hypothesis.original_question: we want to change silver for copper in the fotovoltaic panels

refined.intervention: Replace silver-based photovoltaic metallization with a copper-paste route on crystalline silicon cells, using silver-paste controls and explicit copper-contamination safeguards.
refined.outcome: Maintain initial electrical performance and module-relevant reliability while reducing silver demand.
refined.threshold: Copper-metallized cells should show no more than 0.3 percentage point absolute efficiency loss versus silver-metallized controls and should show no copper-diffusion-linked degradation after reliability screening.
refined.mechanism: Copper reduces silver material intensity and cost pressure, but only works if contact resistance, oxidation, furnace contamination, and copper migration into silicon or module packaging are controlled.
refined.control: Matched crystalline silicon cells or coupons processed with commercial photovoltaic silver conductive paste.

novelty.rationale: The wiki contains direct prior demonstrations of copper paste and copper-plated silicon PV contacts, so the broad idea is not novel. The proposed plan is a focused validation and decision experiment: compare a copper-paste substitution route against a silver-paste control while explicitly testing the failure modes that have limited adoption.

references.title: Thermal Stable High-Efficiency Copper Screen Printed Back Contact Solar Cells
Copper Outdiffusion from Copper-Plated Solar Cell Contacts During Damp Heat Exposure
International Technology Roadmap for Photovoltaic 2024: 15th Edition

summary.budget_confidence: medium-low; specialty paste, cell precursors, SIMS, and reliability chamber costs require vendor or facility quotes
summary.recommended_route: Start with copper paste screen printing rather than a plating-first route, because the wiki has stronger process and reliability support for copper paste and flags a plating-bath procurement gap.

protocol.title: Lock experimental design and procurement gates

reagents.supplier: commercial PV cell vendor or partner lab

failure_warnings.mitigation: Use the copper-paste route for plan-v1; ingest additional supplier sources before attempting a plating-first plan.
Require a dedicated copper firing run, furnace witness wafers, or a documented decontamination sequence before processing silver controls after copper.

protocol.rationale: The wiki supports copper paste as the most Monday-startable route, while the plating route lacks a complete nickel chemistry procurement trail.

protocol.title: Prepare matched silver-control and copper-test lots

reagents.qty: control lot, same layout as copper group where feasible
test lot, matched print geometry target

failure_warnings.mitigation: Use identical layouts where possible and pre-register fill-factor, series-resistance, and line-resistance comparisons.

protocol.rationale: Matched lots isolate the metal substitution from cell-to-cell and layout variation.

protocol.title: Screen print copper paste and silver paste

reagents.qty: print all copper-test cells plus 3 witness coupons
print all control cells plus 3 witness coupons

failure_warnings.mitigation: Follow supplier storage and atmosphere guidance; inspect paste print quality and reject visibly oxidized or discontinuous prints.
Measure finger width, height, line resistance, and post-fire I-V before moving to module stress.

protocol.rationale: Copper paste screen printing is the selected method because it is closest to existing silver-paste workflows and is supported by multiple wiki sources.

protocol.title: Dry, fire, and control furnace cross-contamination

failure_warnings.mitigation: Run copper after baseline silver controls or use a dedicated furnace; include blank witness wafers and a post-copper silver sentinel lot if equipment is shared.
Avoid direct copper-to-silicon contact in the initial plan unless using a validated silver/dielectric barrier geometry; reserve sacrificial cells for SIMS/EDS.

protocol.rationale: The Stradins CRADA report makes furnace contamination a first-order process risk for copper paste adoption.

protocol.title: Initial electrical and contact characterization

failure_warnings.mitigation: Measure I-V, series resistance, fill factor, line resistance, and EL/PL images before stress; stop if copper group misses the 0.3 percentage point efficiency threshold.

protocol.rationale: The refined hypothesis requires performance parity before reliability claims are meaningful.

protocol.title: Copper migration sentinel analysis

failure_warnings.mitigation: Use SIMS depth profiling or SEM/EDS cross-sections on sacrificial samples before encapsulation; do not proceed if copper appears in silicon above control baseline.

protocol.rationale: Karas et al. show that copper migration can be the hidden failure mode even when copper contacts are electrically attractive.

protocol.title: Encapsulate mini-modules for reliability screen

failure_warnings.mitigation: Package matched copper and silver cells; include EVA at minimum and TPO/POE if comparing encapsulant chemistry.
Reserve post-stress cross-sections and SIMS/EDS budget for copper mini-modules.

protocol.rationale: Module packaging can change copper behavior; bare-cell success is not enough for a panel claim.

protocol.title: Run accelerated damp-heat and thermal-cycling gate

failure_warnings.mitigation: Run 85 deg C / 85% RH exposure with early electrical checkpoints; compare copper mini-modules against silver controls.
Add -40 to +85 deg C thermal cycling if chamber access allows; otherwise record as a required follow-on before module-readiness claims.
After stress, perform cross-section EDS/SIMS on copper mini-modules, especially if fill factor or EL changes.

protocol.rationale: The wiki makes damp heat the non-negotiable gate for copper metallization reliability.

protocol.title: Post-stress decision analysis

failure_warnings.mitigation: Only claim commercial value if silver reduction is tied to unchanged performance and reliability metrics.
Reject copper substitution if post-stress series resistance or fill factor degrades beyond silver-control behavior.

protocol.rationale: The final decision must integrate cost/material reduction with the failure map; copper is useful only if it preserves performance and reliability.

materials.item: Matched crystalline silicon PERC cells or precursors
materials.supplier: commercial PV cell vendor or partner lab

budget.line_item: SIMS or equivalent copper depth profiling
Contingency for repeat prints and facility scheduling

timeline.phase: Print, dry, fire, and witness controls

dependencies: Print, dry, fire, and witness controls

timeline.phase: Mini-module encapsulation and sentinel copper analysis

dependencies: Mini-module encapsulation and sentinel copper analysis

primary_success_criteria.criterion: Initial copper-cell efficiency is no more than 0.3 percentage point absolute below the matched silver-paste control.
Copper group shows no copper signal above silver-control baseline in sacrificial SIMS/EDS checks before mini-module stress.
After damp-heat screening, copper mini-modules do not degrade more than matched silver controls in maximum power, fill factor, series resistance, or EL-visible damage.

measurements: fill factor, series resistance, and line resistance
EL or PL imaging before and after stress
SIMS or equivalent copper depth profile on sacrificial and post-stress samples
failure_decision_rules: Stop before encapsulation if initial copper efficiency misses the 0.3 percentage point threshold.
Stop before reliability stress if copper is detected in silicon above control baseline.
Reject module-readiness claim if damp-heat degradation exceeds silver-control behavior or copper outdiffusion is observed.

failure_map.mitigation: Use barrier-aware copper-paste geometry, sacrificial SIMS/EDS checks, and post-stress copper migration analysis.
Run mini-module damp heat, inspect capping/contact regions, and compare EVA versus polyolefin encapsulation if budget allows.
Use dedicated copper firing, furnace witness wafers, or documented decontamination and sentinel controls.
Track line/contact resistance, fill factor, series resistance, and EL images through every phase.
Use matched mini-modules and humidity-stress checkpoints before claiming module-ready copper substitution.

under_supported.gap: Copper paste supplier and catalog number are not present in the current wiki.
under_supported.impact: Budget and procurement lines require quotes before execution.

under_supported.gap: TOPCon-specific copper-plating sources were discovered but not fetched as complete artifacts.
under_supported.impact: Do not choose TOPCon plating as plan-v1 primary route without an incremental ingest.

under_supported.gap: Budget values are planning allowances, not supplier-quoted prices.
under_supported.impact: Treat total budget as an order-of-magnitude execution budget until quotes are attached.

under_supported.gap: Full 1000 h damp-heat reliability cannot complete inside one week.
under_supported.impact: The lab can start on Monday, but module-readiness evidence arrives after the reliability phase.
